title: Repository, Python Runtime, Dependencies, Parsers, and Reproducibility · Contract Workshop
summary: A public-interest site-quality team incrementally builds reproducible crawler repository baseline from original fixed response and HTML fixtures while retaining earlier authorization, HTTP, URL, provenance, politeness, accessibility, testing, security, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of reproducible crawler repository baseline while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, c…
objectives: Inspect revision, status, existing behavior, tests, configuration, data fixtures, licenses, secrets, and a recovery anchor before editing.
Record Python implementation and version, platform, SSL backend, event-loop assumptions, encoding environment, and supported runtime matrix.
Pin direct and resolved dependencies, verify hashes and sources, review licenses and advisories, and prove clean installation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Repository Baseline
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop site-quality team handles case 4072 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile revision, runtime, dependency, parser, license, advisory, and clean-environment evidence; automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance; then change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inspect revision, status, existing behavior, tests, configuration, data fixtures, licenses, secrets, and a recovery anchor before editing.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Repository Baseline.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inspect revision, status, existing behavior, tests, configuration, data fixtures, licenses, secrets, and a recovery anchor before editing. For Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Repository Baseline, keep Repository Baseline connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Environment Reproducibility
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-environment-reproducibility-1e4ij4" that uses case 2650 to reconcile revision, runtime, dependency, parser, license, advisory, and clean-environment evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Environment Reproducibility.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Environment Reproducibility into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Environment Reproducibility, keep Environment Reproducibility connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-environment-reproducibility-1e4ij4
# Competency: scraper-environment-reproducibility.
# Case 2650: an authorized nonprofit content audit.
# Operating constraint: no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def evidence_scraper_environment_reproducibility_1e4ij4(declared_dependencies: int = 5, resolved_dependencies: int = 5, clean_installs: int = 3, parser_profiles: int = 4, unresolved_advisories: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1e4ij4 = "1e4ij4-4ji4e1-2650"
    assert evidence_variant_1e4ij4
    evidence_axes_1e4ij4 = (
        "authorization1e4ij4",
        "url2650",
        "response4ji4e1",
        "tree1e4ij42650",
        "frontier26501e4ij4",
        "report4ji4e12650",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1e4ij4)) == 6
    if declared_dependencies < 1 or resolved_dependencies < 1:
        return False, resolved_dependencies, "dependency-shape"
    if resolved_dependencies != declared_dependencies:
        return False, resolved_dependencies, "dependency-drift"
    if clean_installs < 2 or parser_profiles < 3:
        return False, clean_installs, "reproduction-gap"
    if unresolved_advisories > 0:
        return False, unresolved_advisories, "advisory-gap"
    assert resolved_dependencies == declared_dependencies and clean_installs >= 2
    return True, clean_installs, "reproducible-crawler-baseline"

stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Environment Reproducibility condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
